name: Sant Miquel del Fai - circular gravel through the Bertí cliffs
name_original: Sant Miquel del Fai - circular gravel por los riscos de Bertí
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Bigues i Riells del Fai traverse a diverse landscape in the province of Barcelona, Catalonia. The region features prominent natural landmarks such as the Cingles de Bertí cliffs and the Tenes River valley. Cyclists encounter a mix of hills, lush forests of pine and holm oak, and open green spaces. This varied terrain provides a range of options for touring cyclists, from river-following paths to more challenging climbs. Best touring cycling routes around Bigues i Riells del Fai The most popular touring cycling route is Sant Miquel del Fai - circular gravel through the Bertí cliffs, a 22.9 miles (36.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 22 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers a challenging gravel experience through the dramatic Cingles de Bertí. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Can Cabanyes Natural Area – Via del Colesterol LaGarriga loop from Urbanització el Racó del Bosc, a moderate 32.5 miles (52.2 km) path. This route explores the varied terrain of the Can Cabanyes Natural Area. Local touring cyclists also love the Hermitage of Santa Justa – Scenic Dirt Path loop from Bigues i Riells del Fai, a 23.1 miles (37.1 km) trail leading through scenic dirt paths, often completed in about 2 hours 51 minutes. Touring cycling around Bigues i Riells del Fai is defined by the Cingles de Bertí cliffs, the Tenes River valley, and extensive forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to difficult challenges. The routes in Bigues i Riells del Fai are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 400 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Bigues i Riells del Fai's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Bigues i Riells del Fai?
A: There are over 20 touring cycling routes in Bigues i Riells del Fai, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Bigues i Riells del Fai?
A: The region offers a diverse landscape, from the dramatic &lt;strong&gt;Cingles de Bertí&lt;/strong&gt; cliffs and scenic hills to the more gentle paths along the &lt;strong&gt;Tenes River valley&lt;/strong&gt;. You'll encounter a mix of lush forests, holm oak groves, and open green spaces, providing varied backdrops for your ride.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many routes in Bigues i Riells del Fai are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e976123877/sant-miquel-del-fai-circular-gravel-through-the-berti-cliffs" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Miquel del Fai - circular gravel through the Bertí cliffs&lt;/a&gt; offers a challenging gravel experience, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3986795" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Santa Justa – Scenic Dirt Path loop from Bigues i Riells del Fai&lt;/a&gt; provides a moderate ride through picturesque dirt paths.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of touring cycling routes in Bigues i Riells del Fai?
A: The routes cater to various abilities. While the majority (16 out of 22) are classified as difficult, there are also 5 moderate routes and 1 easy route. This means you can find everything from relaxed rides to challenging climbs requiring very good fitness.

Q: Are there any family-friendly or beginner touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, there is at least one easy route available, and some moderate options like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1479146" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Can Cabanyes Natural Area – Via del Colesterol LaGarriga loop&lt;/a&gt;, which explores varied terrain, can be suitable for families or beginners looking for a longer ride without extreme elevation.

Q: What natural landmarks or viewpoints can I see while cycling?
A: You can enjoy spectacular views from the &lt;strong&gt;Cingles de Bertí&lt;/strong&gt; cliffs and the &lt;strong&gt;Puiggraciós Peak&lt;/strong&gt;. The &lt;strong&gt;Sant Miquel del Fai Natural and Monumental Complex&lt;/strong&gt; is a highlight, featuring unique rock formations and waterfalls. You might also pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2790311" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Montseny Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1478635" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Trona (Bertí Cliffs)&lt;/a&gt; viewpoint.

Q: Are there any historical sites or cultural points of interest along the cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in history. You can visit the ancient monastery at &lt;strong&gt;Sant Miquel del Fai&lt;/strong&gt;, explore the remains of &lt;strong&gt;Montbui Castle&lt;/strong&gt; offering views of the Tenes valley, or cycle towards the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2076587" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puiggraciós Optical Telegraph Tower&lt;/a&gt; for rewarding vistas.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Bigues i Riells del Fai?
A: The routes in Bigues i Riells del Fai are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, the challenging climbs, and the stunning natural beauty, particularly around the Cingles de Bertí.

Q: Is Bigues i Riells del Fai accessible by public transport for cyclists?
A: While specific public transport options for cyclists can vary, the region is part of the Barcelona province, which generally has bus services connecting to smaller municipalities. It's advisable to check local bus schedules and policies regarding bicycles before planning your trip.

Q: Where can I park my car when going for a touring cycle?
A: Parking is typically available in the main towns and villages within Bigues i Riells del Fai, such as Bigues or Riells del Fai. Many routes start from these locations, offering convenient access to trailheads. Look for designated parking areas or public car parks.

Q: Are there any routes that feature waterfalls?
A: Yes, the &lt;strong&gt;Sant Miquel del Fai Natural and Monumental Complex&lt;/strong&gt; is known for its unique landscape shaped by the Tenes and Rossinyol rivers, which create beautiful waterfalls. Routes passing through or near this complex, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e976123877/sant-miquel-del-fai-circular-gravel-through-the-berti-cliffs" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Miquel del Fai - circular gravel through the Bertí cliffs&lt;/a&gt;, will bring you close to these natural wonders.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Bigues i Riells del Fai?
A: The region generally enjoys a Mediterranean climate. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures, making them ideal for cycling. Summers can be hot, especially at midday, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended. Winters are usually mild, but check local weather conditions for cooler days.
